title: El futuro del seguro se juega en la gestión del talento
title_original: El futuro del seguro se juega en la gestión del talento
summary: El sector asegurador afronta uno de los mayores desafíos de su historia en materia de gestión del talento, impulsado por la automatización, el riesgo climático y el cambio en las expectativas de la fuerza laboral. Así lo señala un reciente informe de Aon , que sitúa la construcción de una estrategia de talento preparada para el futuro como una prioridad crítica para las aseguradoras . El rol incluye la gestión de proveedores, supervisión de mantenimientos y apoyo logístico interno para garantizar un entorno de trabajo eficiente y seguro.

title: Del nuevo registro de VPL al deber de información: el sector asegurador se adapta a los cambios legales
title_original: Del nuevo registro de VPL al deber de información: el sector asegurador se adapta a los cambios legales
summary: También destacó el nuevo registro de los VPL según el RD 52/2026. A continuación, José Luis Quilis, responsable de suscripción y siniestros de CGPA , trajo a colación el deber de información de los Mediadores de Seguros subrayando los principios generales de honestidad, imparcialidad y equidad, dando publicidad precisa, clara y no engañosa y evitar conflicto te intereses a la hora de tomar decisiones vía remuneración, que se recogen en el Real Decreto-ley 3/2020 , que “ha supuesto un punto de inflexión en el deber de asesoramiento de los distribuidores de seguros”, concluyó Quilis. Roberto Hernández, director de siniestros de Zurich y Francisco Lisbona, director de Zurich Resilience Solutions , expusieron los retos para el sector asegurador tras la DANA .

title: Siete aseguradoras se suman a ‘La Hora del Planeta’
title_original: Siete aseguradoras se suman a ‘La Hora del Planeta’
summary: El sector asegurador se ha unido un año más a ‘La Hora del Planeta’ , la iniciativa global impulsada por WWF que busca concienciar sobre el cambio climático y fomentar el ahorro y uso eficiente de energía. La iniciativa recuerda problemas ambientales como el calentamiento global y los fenómenos extremos que afectan cada vez con más intensidad a todo el planeta. La acción tuvo lugar el sábado, entre las 20.30 y las 21.30 horas, con el apagado simbólico de luces en oficinas y edificios emblemáticos.

title: LNG Supply Cut Further After Cyclone Hits Australian Plants
title_original: LNG Supply Cut Further After Cyclone Hits Australian Plants
summary: Three Australian plants that provide about 8% of the world’s liquefied natural gas have had their output curbed by a cyclone, in a further blow to mainly Asian buyers reeling from the halt of shipments from Qatar. “Temporary shut-ins at Australian LNG plants come at the worst time for LNG buyers looking to replace supply from Qatar,” said Josh Runciman, lead analyst for Australian gas at the Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis. North West Shelf’s offshore workforce was evacuated in line with Woodside’s cyclone preparation arrangements and production is expected to recommence after they return, according to the company’s spokesperson.

title: HLDI: Safety Benefits Stack up From Driver Assistance Features
title_original: HLDI: Safety Benefits Stack up From Driver Assistance Features
summary: Safety benefits from these driver assistance features also increase as systems get bundled and updated, according to a study from the Highway Loss Data Institute examining the impact of advanced driver assistance systems on Mazda models from 2015 to 2023. The HLDI evaluated several other features which include blind spot detection with rear cross traffic alert, curve-adaptive headlights, heads-up display and traffic sign recognition. Smaller reductions in safety benefits were associated with curve-adaptive headlights and the heads-up display.

title: AM Best upgrades Ocean Re’s credit ratings
title_original: AM Best upgrades Ocean Re’s credit ratings
summary: Ocean International Reinsurance Company Limited (Ocean Re) has had its Financial Strength Rating upgraded to A (Excellent) from A- (Excellent) and its Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating upgraded to “a” (Excellent) from “a-” (Excellent) by AM Best, with the outlook of these ratings revised to stable from positive. AM Best notes that the upgrade reflects Ocean Re’s “very strong” balance sheet strength and operating performance, neutral business profile, and appropriate enterprise risk management. Ocean Re’s business development strategy clearly identifies an increase in the proportion of traditional reinsurance in its portfolio, as compared with its captive portfolio, explained AM Best.

title: Arch Insurance UK Regional Division name Tinworth Senior P&C Underwriter
title_original: Arch Insurance UK Regional Division name Tinworth Senior P&C Underwriter
summary: Henry Tinworth has joined Arch Insurance UK Regional Division as Senior Property & Casualty Underwriter, effective immediately, based in Leeds. His wealth of trading and technical expertise, combined with his service-led approach, will be invaluable as we deepen our broker partnerships, deliver tailored solutions for complex client risks, and strengthen our presence across the region.” Tinworth is joining Arch from DUAL where he was most recently a Senior Underwriter. In his new role, Tinworth will leverage his 10 years of UK insurance marketexperience to drive new business development across the property and casualty portfolio with a regional focus on the north.

title: Converge gets additional cyber insurance capacity from Lloyd’s
title_original: Converge gets additional cyber insurance capacity from Lloyd’s
summary: Converge, a company specialising in cyber risk management and underwriting, has secured additional underwriting capacity from Lloyd’s, the world’s oldest insurance and reinsurance marketplace, for companies with annual revenues up to $5 billion. The expanded capacity from both Lloyd’s and Obsidian reflects strong market confidence in Converge’s strategy of combining rigorous underwriting discipline with advanced technology to achieve better risk selection and pricing precision. The expanded capacity will be available across Converge’s full suite of products, allowing brokers to access comprehensive cyber solutions.”

title: Lines Persist at Some Airports After Trump Signs TSA Pay Memo
title_original: Lines Persist at Some Airports After Trump Signs TSA Pay Memo
summary: Several US airports advised travelers on Saturday to arrive at least four hours before their flights because of long security lines even after President Donald Trump said Transportation Security Administration workers would be paid by tapping funds from his 2025 tax and spending bill. Trump signed a memo Friday directing TSA personnel to be paid as he tried to alleviate disruptions at US airports, but it remains unclear how much it will do to improve wait times at security checkpoints, which have varied widely at different airports. Trump’s memo directed DHS and the White House budget office to use funds that have a “reasonable and logical nexus” to agency operations to provide employees who have worked without pay “with the compensation and benefits that would have accrued to them if not for” the shutdown.

title: Bank of America Agrees to Pay $72.5M to Settle Epstein Accusers’ Lawsuit
title_original: Bank of America Agrees to Pay $72.5M to Settle Epstein Accusers’ Lawsuit
summary: Bank of America agreed to pay $72.5 million to settle a civil lawsuit brought by women who accused the bank of facilitating their sexual abuse by Jeffrey Epstein, court records showed on Friday. Bank of America has said Doe alleged merely that it provided routine services to people who at the time had no known links to Epstein, and that any suggestion that it was more deeply involved was “threadbare and meritless.” Rakoff ruled in January that Bank of America must face Doe’s claims that it knowingly benefited from Epstein’s sex trafficking and obstructed enforcement of the federal Trafficking Victims Protection Act. Doe’s lawyers have also sued other alleged enablers of Epstein’s sex trafficking, and in 2023 reached settlements of $290 million with JPMorgan Chase and $75 million with Deutsche Bank on behalf of his accusers.

title: Trump Unveils New Loan Guarantees for Beleaguered Farmers
title_original: Trump Unveils New Loan Guarantees for Beleaguered Farmers
summary: President Donald Trump announced new loan guarantees for farmers and food suppliers, a bid to bolster US agricultural communities squeezed by the impact of the Iran war, tariffs and other challenges. Trump said the loan guarantees would cover farmers and food suppliers, including vegetable, grain and seed farmers, cattle, pig and poultry producers, as well as grocery wholesalers and others. Trump did not specify a dollar amount for the guarantees.

title: Crawford Contractor Connection partners with Helixco
title_original: Crawford Contractor Connection partners with Helixco
summary: Contractor Connection, a Crawford & Company business that operates a managed repair network, partnered with Helixco , a technology platform designed to capture and validate time and materials documentation in real time across complex commercial repair projects. Contractor Connection will leverage Helixco’s platform to provide real-time visibility into labor, equipment and cost documentation throughout the life of a commercial claim. By integrating Helixco’s platform into our commercial program, we are strengthening consistency, accountability and cost confidence at scale for our carrier partners while supporting efficient execution across our contractor network.” – Lance Malcolm, president, U.S.

title: Investment trust votes, conflicts of interest, and our role
title_original: Investment trust votes, conflicts of interest, and our role
summary: On 3 March 2026, we said we’d bring forward our planned review of the UK Listing Rules for Investment entities, including how they apply to board independence and related party provisions.Since then, there has been substantial debate over our role in relation to investment trusts, including calls for us to ‘get to grips’ with voting rules ‘that allow a minority shareholder to repeatedly attack an investment trust’.Much of this debate suggests there are misunderstandings about how investment trusts are governed and where responsibilities sit. We’re concerned this may confuse investors in these trusts. Other calls to action have lacked clear proposals or been based on future hypothetical scenarios for which protections often exist.

title: FCA fines Dinosaur Merchant Bank Limited for market abuse surveillance failures
title_original: FCA fines Dinosaur Merchant Bank Limited for market abuse surveillance failures
summary: The FCA has fined Dinosaur Merchant Bank Limited (DMBL) £338,000 for failing to put in place effective systems and controls to detect and report suspicious trading in its contracts for difference (CFD) business. CFDs are sophisticated financial products that are used to speculate on various assets going up or down in value. Given their high-risk nature, firms must have strong and reliable surveillance arrangements to prevent insider dealing and market manipulation.In June 2024, DMBL introduced a new order system that led to a sharp increase in CFD trading by its clients.
